title: Energy saving system for an electrical power grid connection
abstract: An energy saving system for an electrical installation is proposed. The system (20) comprises an electrical energy saving device (10) connected to power supply lines (25), the device (10) comprises a housing (6) and a cover (11) with an inner coating of ceramic material (16), electrically conductive bars (1) in an inner cavity (14) connected to respective AC power supply lines; an intermediate plate (15), spaced apart from the cover and from the side walls and separated from the electrically conductive bars (1), and an accumulator layer (18) below the electrically conductive bars. It also includes permanent magnets fixed to the bottom wall (12) and an amplifier layer (19) superimposed on said accumulator layer. The amplifier layer is made of a dielectric polymer gel with a 1,5%-2% contribution of graphene or graphite particles. The accumulator layer comprises PMMA and a 1 %-1.5% contribution of graphene or graphite particles.
claims:
Claims 1. An energy saving system (20) for an electrical power grid connection (30), based on the use of an energy saving device (10) that is connected to an AC electricity line for supplying electric power, in which an inner surface of a housing and an inner cover plate thereof are coated with a ceramic layer that emits and absorbs electromagnetic waves and thereby cause a resonance absorption state when actin on electrically conductive elements placed inside the housing and connected to the electricity line, so that the alternating magnetic field generated by the alternating current flow enters into the housing via the electrically conductive elements and then the magnetic field and electrons interact with the electromagnetic waves to decrease electric power consumption of the power grid connection and save electric energy, the energy saving device (10) comprising: - a housing (6) with a cover (11), side walls (13), and a bottom wall (12), wherein each sidewall (13), bottom wall (12), and cover (11) has an inner face with a coating of ceramic material (16), providing a ceramic radiating surface radiating in the infrared spectrum with wavelengths between 40 and 350 micra; - at least two electrically conductive bars (1) arranged in the inner cavity (14) attached at their ends to electrically insulating supports (2) and connected by electric conductors (8) via one end to respective AC power supply lines (25), allowing the electrons in the power supply stream to fill or be incorporated into such electrically conductive bars (1); - an intermediate plate (15) with a flat configuration supported on columns (9), arranged in the inner cavity (14) spaced apart from the cover (11) and located at a pre-determined distance from the electrically conductive bars (1), the side edges of said intermediate plate (15) being spaced apart from the side walls (13); and - an accumulator layer (18) including a polymer compound which occupies an inner cavity (14) demarcated between said bottom (12) and side walls (13), with a surface thereof being at a level lower than said electrically conductive bars (1); characterized in that it further comprises: - at least a group of permanent magnets fixed to the bottom wall (12) and arranged at a distance from the electrically conductive bars (1), configured for generating transverse magnetic fields with respect to the electrically conductive bars (1); and - an amplifier layer (19) in which said electrically conductive bars (1) are embedded, said amplifier layer (19) comprising a dielectric polymer gel with a 1.5% to 

 2% contribution of graphene or graphite particles, configured for amplifying the electromagnetic waves emanated from the ceramic layer; wherein said accumulator layer (18) is formed by a chemical compound comprising poly(methyl methacrylate), PMMA, and a 1% to 1.5% contribution of graphene or graphite particles, and wherein said intermediate plate (15) is provided, on its upper face facing the cover, (11), with a layer of spheroid ceramic material (17) configured to reflect and distributing magnetic fields generated inside the housing (6) closed with the cover (11). 2. The system (20) according to claim 1 , comprising four electrically conductive copper bars with a flat configuration, with a purity comprised between 90% and 100%, preferably of 99.9%, connected to each of the phases of a three-phase power supply lines (25) and to the neutral conductor. 3. The system (20) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the housing (6), the cover (11), and the plate (15) have a rectangular layout and the dimensions of length (x), width (y), and height (z) of the plate (15), the accumulator layer (18), and the amplifier layer (19) satisfy the following ratio: z = y Â· f f being the golden ratio defined by the expression: 4. The system (20) according to claim 2, comprising two parallel spaced-apart alignments of permanent magnets, a first of said alignments being located below the electrically conductive bars (1) and a second alignment of permanent magnets being located outside the area covered by the electrically conductive bars (1). 5. The system (20) according to claim 4, wherein the first alignment of permanent magnets comprises four permanent neodymium magnets (3A) with a cylindrical configuration and the second alignment of permanent magnets comprises three permanent ferritic magnets (3B). 6. The system (20) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the side walls (13), the cover (11), and the bottom (12) of the housing (6) are metal parts made of steel, externally coated with oven-baked paint. 7. The system (20) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the intermediate plate (15) comprises a core made of grain-oriented Fe-Si steel sheet of between 1 and 4 mm thick. 8. The system (20) according to claim 1, wherein said coating of ceramic material (16) of the inner faces of the side walls (13), the intermediate plate (15), the bottom wall (12), and the cover (11) are made of an epoxy-coated, abrasion-resistant, low-friction material.
